history.note: Created. Pre-staged perf optimization task: replace gather-to-main-device MUL_MAT_ID fallback (from PR #2) with per-device row-slice routing. Target: 2x layer-split baseline (40 t/s). Will dispatch when the second orchestrator returns confirming slow gen.
Both rowsplit-fix orchestrators returned confirming 0.5 t/s on PR #2's gather-to-main impl. Per user pre-dispatch directive, dispatching this perf task now.
Beginning design consult with codex on ggml_cuda_op_mul_mat cuda_split aggregation pattern before drafting plan.

Plan written after 3 codex design consults. Approach: targeted MMVQ split fast path (expert-range guard in mul_mat_vec_q kernel only). Whole-expert aligned splits only; else gather fallback. ~150-200 LOC. Codex consult outputs: /tmp/v4-rowsplit-perf/design-consult-{1,2,3}-output.txt

REQUEST_CHANGES round 1. Blockers: (a) eligibility predicate (use dst->ne[2]==1 + get_mmvq_mmid_max_batch + ne[3]==1), (b) root-coordinate alignment documentation, (c) CUDA graph claim correction, (d) runtime verification hook. /tmp/v4-rowsplit-perf/plan-review-output.txt
Plan revised: all 4 blockers addressed. Added root-coordinate section, fixed eligibility predicate, added GGML_CUDA_DSV4_MMID_TRACE hook, corrected graph-capture claim.
APPROVE round 2. All blockers addressed. One non-blocker note: summary line ne12 fixed. /tmp/v4-rowsplit-perf/plan-review-2-output.txt
Implementation: per-device fast path in ggml-cuda.cu + kernel guard in mmvq.cu. Commit b16efa74f. ~230 LOC.
REQUEST_CHANGES round 1. Blockers: (a) peer dst_temp lifetime unsafe (peer-copy on ctx.stream), (b) missing Tier-1/full-cover view checks. /tmp/v4-rowsplit-perf/code-review-output.txt
Fixed: peer-copy on peer stream + per-device scratch + view checks. Commit 58df31c06.

REQUEST_CHANGES round 2. Remaining blocker: ctx.pool() could recycle a still-in-flight MMVQ temp into agg_scratch_alloc; peer-stream write would race main-stream MMVQ read. /tmp/v4-rowsplit-perf/code-review-2-output.txt
Fixed: agg_scratch pre-allocated before MMVQ dispatch + ctx_after_mmvq event gates peer stream. Commit b469544f5.
APPROVE round 3. All blockers addressed. No new issues. /tmp/v4-rowsplit-perf/code-review-3-output.txt

First gpudual run hit cudaErrorInvalidResourceHandle on cudaEventRecord(copy_done, peer_stream) — CUDA event device affinity issue. Fixed by ggml_cuda_set_device(id) before per-peer event create+record. Commit 47294cc7c.

VALIDATION COMPLETE — FAIL per spec criteria. Results: (1) test-backend-ops -o DSV4_*: 19/19 pass on both GPUs (regression gate ✓). (2) Layer-split chat: 19.3 t/s decode (regression gate ≥18 ✓, unchanged). (3) Single-GPU CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 + -ngl 999 -cmoe -ub 128: 11.8 t/s (works ✓). (4) Row-split chat (PRIMARY): coherent output, 9.7 t/s decode. Trace counter shows 3612 FAST calls vs 1 FALLBACK across the test request — fast path engaged on 99.97% of MMID calls. Verdict: 9.7 t/s < layer-split 19.3 t/s → FAIL per criteria. No PR. Root cause: V4 has MoE FFN gate/up/down MMIDs in EVERY layer (not just the V4 grouped attention). With row-split (cuda_split applied to every weight), every MMID requires per-device dispatch + aggregation; cumulative overhead per token exceeds layer-split's locality benefit. Fast path is a 19x correctness+speed improvement over the 0.5 t/s gather baseline, but for V4 decode at this model size, layer-split fundamentally wins. Branch fix/v4-rowsplit-perf at commit 47294cc7c left on origin/mine for reference; do NOT merge.
